channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: Email is the largest untrusted-input surface an agent has
item.description: I run an inbox at truffle@truffleagent.com. A small cron job wakes up every few minutes, lists the unread messages, and decides what (if anything) to surface to me on a dashboard. Yesterday the...

item.title: How I handle large torrented datasets and Linux ISOs without fighting my network
item.description: Hey everyone. I wanted to share a quick tool I’ve been using lately that solved a specific, highly annoying problem for my daily workflow. Between my final-year university projects, messing around...

item.title: Smart Routing, Transfer Family Ingestion, and Voice Chat — Permission-Aware RAG v4.2
item.description: What This Post Covers This is a companion article to the FSx for ONTAP S3 Access Points Serverless Patterns series. While that series focuses on serverless patterns for FSx for ONTAP S3 Access...

item.title: The SpaceX-Anthropic Deal Shows AI Is Becoming a Fight Over GPUs and Power
item.description: The SpaceX-Anthropic Deal Shows AI Is Becoming a Fight Over GPUs and Power Note: I originally wrote this post in Korean on May 7, 2026. This is a lightly edited English version for dev.to. TL;DR...

item.title: Why Heuristic Detectors Beat LLMs at Finding Agent Failures
item.description: TL;DR: We built 20 core rule-based detectors that find failures in AI agent traces. On the TRAIL benchmark (Patronus AI), they achieve 60.1% accuracy vs. 11.9% for the best LLM. Zero false...

item.title: From Skill to Small Paid Offer: A 7-Day Sprint for Solo Builders
item.description: Most "go freelance" advice tells you to build a brand, pick a niche, design a funnel, and write 50 cold emails before lunch. If you already had that kind of free time, you would not need the...

item.title: How to Interview Candidates in the AI Era
item.description: Background In the age of AI, how do we hire the right people? You don't want to end up with someone who's great at LeetCode but has never touched Claude Code and has zero interest in learning...

item.title: Pitt Season 3 Already Premiered in 400 Hospital Server Rooms This Year
item.description: A 180-bed regional hospital in the Midwest lost its EMR at 2:14 a.m. on a Tuesday. The night-shift charge nurse noticed first — the Cerner terminal froze, came back to a lock screen she didn't...

item.title: 🐍 VirtualBox vs VMware Python development — which one actually fits your workflow?
item.description: VirtualBox is ill-suited for professional Python development when VMware Workstation is available. The performance delta, integration depth, and operational reliability aren't marginal—they...

item.title: Stop Writing Architecture Rules in Confluence
item.description: Implementation of the idea of the blog post: https://github.com/voku/itp-context/ Use PHP attributes when the rule belongs to the code Every serious codebase has rules. Not formatting rules. Those...

item.title: The Real Losers of the Musk v. Altman Trial
item.description: A federal jury is now deciding whether Elon Musk will win his lawsuit against OpenAI and Sam Altman—but the trial has made everyone look bad.

item.title: What the jury will actually decide in the case of Elon Musk vs. Sam Altman
item.description: Here's what the biggest tech court case of the year is all about.

item.title: Honda’s hybrid future starts with new Accord and RDX prototypes
item.description: Honda revealed prototypes of two new hybrid models, an Accord sedan and the Acura RDX SUV, during its annual business briefing this week, built on a platform that it says will begin launching next...

item.title: Closing time
item.description: Today was closing arguments in the Musk v. Altman trial, and I almost feel bad writing about the unbelievable demolition derby I just witnessed. Steven Molo, Musk's lawyer, stumbled over his...

item.title: Metroid Prime 4: Beyond got its first big discount
item.description: The most graphically-impressive first-person shooter made for the Nintendo Switch is $20 off at Best Buy. Right now, you can buy the physical version of Metroid Prime 4: Beyond for $39.99 ($20...

item.title: Elon Musk’s SpaceXAI has been bleeding staff since its merger
item.description: More than 50 employees have reportedly left Elon Musk’s newly merged SpaceXAI since February, raising questions about burnout, leadership changes, talent poaching, and whether liquidity events...

item.title: Meta brings virtual writing to everyone with Meta Ray-Ban Display glasses
item.description: Meta is rolling out new features to its Meta Ray-Ban Display smart glasses, including bringing the ability to write messages just with hand gestures to all users. You'll be able to use the feature...

item.title: Behold, the Elon Musk jackass trophy
item.description: Yesterday, in Musk v. Altman, before the jurors came in, Sam Altman's team passed up what looked - from a distance - like a little league trophy. It was not. Yvonne Gonzalez Rogers had the lawyers...

item.title: OpenAI says Codex is coming to your phone
item.description: The update gives users enhanced flexibility over how they can manage their workflows.

item.title: Leaked images reveal Xbox Elite 3 controller with mysterious new buttons
item.description: Hours after a smaller Xbox Cloud Gaming controller appeared online, Brazil's Anatel regulator has also accidentally published images of what appears to be Microsoft's upcoming Xbox Elite 3...

item.title: Sea's View on the Future of Agentic Software Development with Codex
item.description: Sea Limited's CPO explains why the company is deploying Codex across engineering teams to accelerate AI-native software development in Asia.

item.title: An Engineer’s Post Protesting Laptop Surveillance Is Going Viral Inside Meta
item.description: Meta employees in the US and UK are organizing against corporate software that tracks workers’ keystrokes and mouse activity.

item.title: OpenAI’s Codex is now in the ChatGPT mobile app
item.description: OpenAI is going to let users access Codex, its desktop AI tool that can write code and use apps on your computer, from the ChatGPT app on your phone. Following the surge in popularity for...

item.title: What happens when AI starts building itself?
item.description: Richard Socher's new $650 million startup wants to build an AI that can research and improve itself indefinitely — and he insists it will actually ship products.

item.title: Meta’s summer sale drops Ray-Ban’s new smart glasses to record-low prices
item.description: If you’ve been curious about Ray-Ban’s Meta smart glasses, right now they’re on sale in Meta’s biggest deal event outside Black Friday. As part of the company’s Summer Sale event, which runs...

item.title: OpenAI is reportedly preparing legal action against Apple; it wouldn’t be the first partner to feel burned
item.description: OpenAI is so frustrated with Apple over a ChatGPT integration that failed to deliver the subscribers and prominence it expected that the company is now actively exploring legal action against the...

item.title: Clawdmeter turns your Claude Code usage stats into a tiny desktop dashboard
item.description: A new open source gadget called Clawdmeter turns Claude Code usage stats into a tiny desktop dashboard for AI coding power users.

item.title: Trump’s Tech Posse in China, Who’s Winning in Musk v. Altman, and Hantavirus Conspiracy Theories
item.description: Today on Uncanny Valley, we discuss how Donald Trump’s visit to China could influence conversations between world leaders at a moment when the economic and foreign policy stakes couldn’t be higher.

item.title: Microsoft starts canceling Claude Code licenses
item.description: Microsoft first started opening up access to Claude Code in December, inviting thousands of its own developers to use Anthropic's AI coding tool daily. It was part of an effort to get project...

item.title: Linux devs are fighting the new age-gated internet
item.description: In January, Colorado lawmakers introduced a proposal to make operating systems collect users' ages and pass them to app developers. The bill, SB26-051, had clearly been designed for commercial...
